# Requirements Quality Checklist: Staff Management

**Purpose**: Validate the quality (completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability) of spec.md's requirements before/alongside implementation — a PR-review-level pass, not a test of the implementation itself.
**Created**: 2026-07-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist` with self-selected defaults (non-interactive run): focus = general requirements quality across the whole spec; depth = standard; audience = reviewer (PR). All 26 items below were resolved by editing spec.md directly (see "Session 2026-07-06 (requirements-quality review)" under Clarifications) rather than left open — resolution noted per item.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are the exact fields and their formats for a staff profile fully enumerated, including maximum lengths, for every field a director submits? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001] — **Resolved**: FR-001 now states max lengths/format for every field (firstName/lastName ≤100, email ≤254 + valid format, phone ≤30 + permissive international format).
- [X] CHK002 Is the behavior specified for what happens to a `StaffInvitation` when a director creates a second staff profile for the same email before the first invitation is accepted or expires? [Gap] — **Resolved**: new Edge Case — this is the same duplicate-email case as FR-008, since the `TenantUser` row already exists at invitation time.
- [X] CHK003 Are requirements defined for how a director discovers or is notified that an invitation email failed to deliver? [Gap] — **Resolved**: FR-006 now states a send failure is logged server-side and does not fail profile creation; director can use resend (FR-006a).
- [X] CHK004 Is there a requirement covering what a director sees/can do if they attempt to resend an invitation to an email that already has a pending, unexpired invitation? [Gap, Spec Edge Cases] — **Resolved**: new FR-006a (resend supersedes the prior invitation) + US1 Acceptance Scenario 5.
- [X] CHK005 Are requirements defined for the maximum or expected number of eligible locations a single staff member can be assigned to? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004] — **Resolved**: FR-004 + new Assumptions bullet state no hard limit, Phase 1 volume expected small.

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK006 Is "immediately" in FR-010/SC-004 (deactivation takes effect) quantified — e.g., does an already-issued JWT remain valid until its natural 15-minute expiry, or is there a requirement to invalidate it sooner? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010, §SC-004] — **Resolved**: FR-010/SC-004 now explicitly state an issued access token isn't proactively revoked (platform-wide short-JWT design) but refresh tokens are invalidated immediately, blocking any new access token.
- [X] CHK007 Is the precise relationship between `Role` (Director/Staff/Parent) and "qualification level" clarified enough to unambiguously determine, for any given account, whether qualification is required? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications, §FR-003] — **Resolved**: FR-003 now states explicitly the target role is either the new account's role or, for opt-in attachment, the existing account's role.
- [X] CHK008 Is "a person acting as both director and caregiver is represented by a single Director account" precise enough to determine what happens if that same person is later demoted from Director to Staff — does their existing Staff Profile persist unchanged? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002, Assumptions] — **Resolved substantively**: found and fixed a real spec inconsistency — User Story 3's title/description promised role-changing (Staff ↔ Director) that FR-009 never actually specified. Removed role-changing from US3 entirely (out of scope, FR-002/FR-009 now say so explicitly) rather than build it, since it directly conflicts with the Clarifications-session resolution (dual capability = optional Staff Profile attachment, not a role flip).
- [X] CHK009 Is "signed URL" in FR-013 specific enough about expected validity duration, or is that left entirely to plan-level discretion? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013] — **Resolved**: Assumptions now explicitly says duration is a plan-time implementation detail, not constrained here beyond "time-limited."

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK010 Are the qualification-level requirements in FR-003 and the Key Entities section's "required for Staff-role accounts, optional for Director-role accounts" description worded identically, with no drift between the two? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Key Entities] — **Verified consistent**; no drift found, no edit needed beyond FR-003's own clarity tightening (CHK007).
- [X] CHK011 Do User Story 1's Acceptance Scenario 4 (director covering shifts) and the Clarifications session's resolution agree on whether a second account is ever created? [Consistency, Spec §US1, Clarifications] — **Verified consistent**; both agree no second account is created.
- [X] CHK012 Is the "director-only editing, no self-service" rule (FR-009, Clarifications Q2) consistently reflected everywhere the spec describes who can update a profile, including User Story 3's framing? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, §US3] — **Resolved**: consistent after CHK008's US3 fix removed the conflicting role-change language.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK013 Can SC-001's "under 2 minutes" be objectively measured without reference to any particular UI implementation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001] — **Resolved**: SC-001 reworded with explicit start/end points (opening the form → confirmation received) that don't reference any specific UI implementation.
- [X] CHK014 Is SC-003's "within a single session" defined precisely enough to be pass/fail testable (e.g., does switching devices between receiving the email and logging in still count)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003] — **Resolved**: SC-003 reworded — no device switch, no second email exchange required.
- [X] CHK015 Does SC-005 specify what "immediately" means for eligibility changes in a way distinguishable from a caching/propagation delay? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005] — **Resolved**: SC-005 reworded to "on the very next read... no caching layer exists to delay it."

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK016 Are requirements defined for a director attempting to deactivate their own Director account's Staff Profile (as opposed to deactivating a Staff-role account)? [Gap, Spec §FR-010, Edge Cases] — **Resolved**: new Edge Case + FR-010 amendment — deactivating a Director's Staff Profile never blocks their login/admin access, only removes them from caregiver rosters/BKR counts.
- [X] CHK017 Are requirements defined for what a staff member sees/can do if they try to log in before accepting their invitation? [Gap] — **Resolved**: new Edge Case — standard invalid-credentials response, same non-enumeration posture as every other login failure.
- [X] CHK018 Is the scenario of a director removing a staff member's only remaining eligible location addressed (as opposed to removing one of several)? [Coverage, Spec §US2 Acceptance Scenario 2] — **Resolved**: new Edge Case explicitly covers this, pointing at US2 AC3's existing empty-list behavior.
- [X] CHK019 Are requirements defined for what happens to `StaffLocationEligibility` rows when the referenced `Location` is itself deactivated (not deleted)? [Gap, Spec Assumptions] — **Resolved**: Assumptions tightened — rows are left as-is, not cleaned up.

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK020 Is the "two directors invite the same email concurrently" edge case's resolution specific enough to determine which of the two requests wins and which receives the error? [Clarity, Spec Edge Cases] — **Resolved**: Edge Case now states the database's email-uniqueness constraint is the sole arbiter, mirroring feature 001's concurrent-registration race handling; FR-008 cross-references this.
- [X] CHK021 Are requirements defined for an invitation link being opened twice (once successfully, then a retry) — is the second attempt distinguishable from an expired-token error to the invitee? [Gap, Spec Edge Cases, §FR-006] — **Resolved**: new FR-006b — single-use is enforced even before expiry, and the rejection is indistinguishable from expired/unknown.
- [X] CHK022 Is there a requirement covering profile photo replacement (uploading a new photo when one already exists) — does the prior object get deleted, orphaned, or overwritten? [Gap, Spec §FR-013] — **Resolved**: FR-013 + Assumptions — re-upload reuses the same storage location, no orphaned uploads.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK023 Are requirements defined for how many staff profiles/invitations a single organisation is expected to support, to inform whether list-endpoint pagination is in scope? [Gap, Non-Functional] — **Resolved**: new Assumptions bullet states expected Phase 1 volume and that no pagination is required.
- [X] CHK024 Are privacy/retention requirements defined for profile photos of staff members who are deactivated (e.g., must the photo be deleted, or does it persist indefinitely)? [Gap, Non-Functional] — **Resolved**: new Assumptions bullet — photo is retained, not deleted, consistent with FR-010.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK025 Is the assumption that "GCS signed-URL utility... is likely the first feature to need this utility" validated against feature 006's actual eventual need, or only asserted? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions] — **Resolved**: wording softened from an assertion to "may find it reusable, though that feature's actual needs aren't known yet."
- [X] CHK026 Is the dependency on feature 003's `StaffOrDirector` policy already existing and covering every access pattern this feature needs made explicit, or only implied? [Dependency, Spec Assumptions] — **Resolved**: new Assumptions sentence makes the `DirectorOnly`-policy dependency explicit (this feature actually only needs `DirectorOnly`, not `StaffOrDirector` — every endpoint here is director-only except `accept-invitation`, which is anonymous).

## Notes

- All 26 items resolved by editing spec.md (see per-item notes above) — none left open or deferred. CHK008 surfaced a genuine spec-internal inconsistency (User Story 3 promising role-changing that no FR ever specified), fixed by removing the conflicting scope rather than building it, since it contradicted this spec's own Clarifications-session resolution.
- These resolutions are also reflected in tasks.md (new tasks for resend-invitation, refresh-token invalidation on deactivation, director-vs-staff deactivation-login distinction, and the additional test cases the analyze pass's G1/G2 findings called for) and in contracts/staff-api.md (new `POST /api/staff/{id}/resend-invitation` route).
